tluaTypeofRemoved.tlua(12,1): error TS1128: Declaration or statement expected.
tluaTypeofRemoved.tlua(14,13): error TS1109: Expression expected.
tluaTypeofRemoved.tlua(16,4): error TS1109: Expression expected.
tluaTypeofRemoved.tlua(16,25): error TS1005: ';' expected.
tluaTypeofRemoved.tlua(17,9): error TS2322: Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
  Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.


==== tluaTypeofRemoved.tlua (5 errors) ====
    // The `typeof` OPERATOR does not exist in tlua: `type(value)` is the Lua type
    // guard, and the operator has no AST node left to parse into. What follows is a
    // syntax error, not a checked expression.
    //
    // Each use below also pins that the parser keeps making progress: `typeof` does
    // not start an expression, so the statement list skips the token instead of
    // re-entering on it forever.
    
    declare v: string | number;
    declare function f(a: number): string;
    
    typeof v;
    ~~~~~~
!!! error TS1128: Declaration or statement expected.
    
    local tag = typeof v;
                ~~~~~~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
    
    if typeof v == "string" then
       ~~~~~~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
                            ~~~~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
      local s: string = v;
            ~
!!! error TS2322: Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
!!! error TS2322:   Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
    end
    
    // `typeof` TYPE QUERIES are static syntax with no runtime counterpart, and stay.
    local g: typeof f = f;
    type Q = typeof v;
    local r: string = g(1);
